own_review.point: Behavioral regression: with IS_NOT_DISTINCT_FROM constant inference, equi-join keys bound to constants are eliminated from join conditions; queries like testInnerJoinWithJoinConditionPushDown can no longer be planned as Broadcast/Shuffled-Hash/SortMerge joins and now throw TableException 'Cannot generate a valid execution plan' (new overrides in BroadcastHashJoinTest/ShuffledHashJoinTest/SortMergeJoinTest.scala). Previously-working queries hard-fail for users with table.exec.disabled-operators=NestedLoopJoin, or silently degrade to NestedLoopJoin. PR description does not mention this user-facing impact.

Streaming plan regressions visible in JoinTest.xml: join input spec degrades from JoinKeyContainsUniqueKey to NoUniqueKey (larger join state, weaker changelog semantics), and one plan becomes where=[true] with Exchange(distribution=[single]) on both sides, i.e. a parallelism-1 cross join.

DecimalITCase testJoin1-7: removing TABLE_EXEC_DISABLED_OPERATORS='HashJoin, NestedLoopJoin' silently drops the SortMergeJoin coverage for decimal-key joins that the setting existed to force (it had to be removed because those joins can no longer be planned as SortMergeJoin after constant inference).

Stale class Javadoc: header still says the class was copied because IS NOT DISTINCT FROM constant inference 'clashes with filter push down', which this PR re-enables; only the CALCITE-6764 backport remains as a modification. Also the updated range 'Line 2489~2494' is off by one (markers at 2489-2493).

Stale comment in DynamicFunctionPlanTestBase.testAggregateReduceConstants2 claims SUBSTR(CAST(LOCALTIME AS VARCHAR),1,2) 'will never be inferred as constant' - the new batch plan now infers exactly that (hh dropped from groupBy, LOCALTIME re-evaluated in a post-aggregate Calc). Re-evaluating a dynamic function in a different operator is only safe because batch evaluates time functions at query start; streaming behavior should be explicitly verified.

No new test exercises an explicit IS NOT DISTINCT FROM predicate; all changes are golden-plan updates for '=' queries (which hit the new path only via RelMdPredicates.eqConstant generating INDF for nullable columns). A direct test of the changed code path, and a regression test demonstrating the original filter-push-down clash is fixed, are missing.

Stream plans degrade to NoUniqueKey / single-distribution cross join

qualitative: v3 is clearly the best: it found the stale fork Javadoc, the missing explicit IS NOT DISTINCT FROM test, the RelMdPredicates.eqConstant trigger mechanism, and at least asked the right correctness question about LOCALTIME in DynamicFunctionPlanTest, though it fabricated one specific (claiming the unchanged FOJ test case changed). Strikingly, all three candidates missed the single point the human reviewer made and the diff loudly advertises: constant inference now erases equi-join keys, so hash/sort-merge joins can no longer be planned for such queries - three test overrides were added expecting 'Cannot generate a valid execution plan' and DecimalITCase had to drop its forced-SortMergeJoin config. v2 is the worst offender: it declared the PR 'safe, low-risk' and claimed test changes 'show improved plans' and that 'no new tests were added', directly contradicting the regression-acknowledging test changes in the diff. v4 sits in between with solid documentation points but it blessed the plan changes as 'removing deterministic expressions' when the expression in question (LOCALTIME) is dynamic, inverting the actual risk. None of the candidates noticed the streaming-side degradation to NoUniqueKey join specs and single-parallelism exchanges.
